section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional

check_details.13.1_detail: Scanned artifact content and traceability_footer.primary_sources for references to Grant Agreement Annex, AGA Template, or Model Grant Agreement template used as structural authority. None found. Primary sources reference Tier 2A instrument schemas (section_schema_registry.json, evaluator_expectation_registry.json), Tier 2B call sources, Tier 3 architecture inputs, and Tier 4 phase outputs — all appropriate application-form-aligned sources.
check_details.13.2_detail: Artifact references call expected impacts (EI-01 through EI-05). traceability_footer.primary_sources includes docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_impacts.json (tier 2) and docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_outcomes.json (tier 2). Individual claim_statuses also reference Tier 2B sources (e.g. CL-04 references expected_impacts.json:EI-04). All call-constraint claims carry Tier 2B source references.
check_details.13.3_detail: Artifact names partners NIHS, ISIA, ELI, ATU, BIIS, CERIA, FIIT, BAL (Boreal AI Labs Oy) with specific roles and capabilities. traceability_footer.primary_sources includes three paths within docs/tier3_project_instantiation/: architecture_inputs/objectives.json, outcomes.json, and impacts.json. Individual claim source_refs reference Tier 3 sources (e.g. impacts.json:IMP-2,IMP-3,IMP-4; objectives.json:OBJ-1,OBJ-2,OBJ-3). Tier 3 source references are present for project fact claims.
check_details.13.4_detail: Artifact schema_id is orch.tier5.impact_section.v1 (begins with orch.tier5.), confirming it is a Phase 8 artifact. Content scanned for budget figure patterns (€[0-9]+, [0-9]+ EUR, [0-9]+ person-months as confirmed values): none found. Percentage figures present (10%, 15%, 20%, 25%, 30%, 40%) are KPI performance targets, not budget figures. No budget-dependent claims detected in artifact content. Note: budget gate assessment file existence could not be independently verified from within this skill's declared input boundary (CLAUDE.md and artifact_path only); content-level budget dependency check passed.
check_details.13.5_detail: Not directly checkable from artifact content. This prohibition concerns agent runtime behavior (decisions held in memory vs. written to Tier 4). Enforcement is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill and agent runtime. Pass by non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
check_details.13.6_detail: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition concerns workflow/skill scope expansion. Pass by non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
check_details.13.7_detail: Artifact is a Phase 8 deliverable that references Phase 5 (impact_architecture.json) and Phase 3 (wp_structure.json) as upstream inputs. This is the correct phase dependency direction — Phase 8 consuming earlier phase outputs. No evidence of referencing outputs from phases that should execute after Phase 8 (no such phases exist). No evidence of skipped phase dependencies.
check_details.13.8_detail: validation_status.overall_status is 'inferred' (not 'unresolved'). Three claims (CL-04, CL-09, CL-10) have status 'inferred' — each has a stated source_ref identifying the inference chain. No claims have status 'unresolved'. The §13.8 violation condition (unresolved status present but not explicitly flagged) is not triggered. Inferred claims are properly documented per §12.2 semantics.
check_details.13.9_detail: Content scanned for claims about Horizon Europe participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation requirements. References to 'AI Act compliance' (content of WP5 activity) and 'ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42' (standardisation pathway) are project activities, not claims about Horizon Europe programme rules. No Horizon Europe regulatory claims requiring Tier 1 source references were identified.
check_details.13.10_detail: traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. All 10 claim_statuses entries have non-empty source_ref fields referencing Tier 2, 3, or 4 sources. traceability_footer.primary_sources lists 9 source paths across Tiers 2, 3, and 4. No unattributed claims detected.
check_details.13.11_detail: Not checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition concerns modification of source documents in Tier 1 or Tier 2 directories. Pass by non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
check_details.13.12_detail: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition concerns the treatment of CLAUDE.md by agents and processes. Pass by non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
